rulings.Case Title: JOHN VALDEZ, AN INDIVIDUAL, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF VS. GARIB KARAPETYAN, A/K/A JUSTIN KARAPETYAN, AN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ronald Foreman; Foreman & Brassos Second Amended Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Monday, May 12, 2025 Line 12, DEFENDANT RONALD FOREMAN, FOREMAN & BRASSO Second Amended Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Continued to June 3, 2025, on the court's motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: KYLE BLAQUIERE VS. FREEWHEEL LLC DBA MATTINA, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-619824 WITH CASE CGC-24-619796
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Monday, May 12, 2025 Line 7, DEFENDANT MATTHEW ACCARRINO , AN INDIVIDUAL, FREEWHEEL LLC MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-619824 WITH CASE CGC-24-619796. Defendants Matthew Accarrino and Freewheel LLC ' (MATTHEW ACCARRINO and FREEWHEEL LLC) motion to consolidate for all purposes (complete consolidation) is denied. "There are two types of consolidation: a complete consolidation resulting in a single action, and a consolidation of separate actions for trial. Under the former procedure, which may be utilized where the parties are identical and the causes of action should have been joined, the pleadings are regarded as merged, one set of findings is made, and one judgment is rendered. In a consolidation for trial, the pleadings, verdicts, findings and judgments are kept separate; the actions are simply tried together for the sake of convenience and judicial economy." (Sanchez v. Superior Court (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1391, 1396.) When ruling on a motion to consolidate the court should consider the common issues of law and fact, timeliness (trial date/discovery), complexity of the cases, and prejudice to the parties. CGC-24-619796 involves an eleven-count complaint alleging wage and hour violations, retaliation, etc. with regard to plaintiff Blaquiere's employment at the Mattina restaurant. CGC-24-619824 involves a fourteen-count complaint alleging wage and hour violations, retaliation, disability discrimination, etc. while he worked at the SPQR restaurant. While there is an overlap in witnesses (e.g. plaintiff and Accarrino) complete consolidation is improper under Sanchez and would lead to an unwieldy action. Plaintiff's claims are distinct as to each employer and CGC-24-619796 has no issues regarding disability discrimination. Complete consolidation would lead to jury confusion, a byzantine summary judgment motion, etc. The issues of prejudice and confusion outweigh any judicial economy that will be furthered by consolidation. The court orders the parties to coordinate discovery and other motion practice to prevent duplication of effort and to conserve judicial and party resources. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
